overview: This Pioneer Azorius deck revolves around casting Metalwork Colossus for free or cheap using high-mana-value artifacts like Brass Knuckles and Leyline Axe to achieve a double-strike lethal attack. It features an alternative win condition via Simulacrum Synthesizer tokens and includes board interaction and recursion tools to protect the combo. The deck aims to assemble its pieces quickly, bypass blockers with Wedding Invitation, and loop back to hand if removal is encountered.
win_conditions: Metalwork Colossus equipped with Brass Knuckles and/or Leyline Axe for a double-strike lethal attack
Simulacrum Synthesizer tokens equipped with Leyline Axe
Ingenious Smith as a complementary threat if it stays on the board

keep_criteria: Hands containing Leyline Axe and early artifact enablers like Brass Knuckles or Moonsnare Prototype
Hands with multiple high-mana-value artifacts to trigger Simulacrum Synthesizer
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Simulacrum Synthesizer, and at least one early artifact or cantrip.
Drop hands with three or more lands and no early plays, or hands with only Metalwork Colossus and no way to cast it.
Keep hands with Leyline Axe if paired with early mana and a target artifact.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early mana or artifact reduction tools
Hands heavy on lands or late-game recursion without early setup

examples.reason: Leyline Axe is explicitly noted as the best card for the starting hand, and combined with Brass Knuckles and early mana, it enables a fast Colossus play.

early_game: Play Leyline Axe on turn one if possible to establish free mana and cost reduction
Use Moonsnare Prototype to ramp and protect Leyline Axe
Search for artifacts with Ingenious Smith
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cheap artifact or cantrip.
use its enter-the-battlefield trigger to put a +1/+1 counter on Leyline Axe or another artifact, or sacrifice it later to fuel Colossus.
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit your two-drop and six-drop without flooding.
mid_game: Cast Metalwork Colossus for free or cheap on turn three or four
Equip with Brass Knuckles and Leyline Axe for double strike
Use Wedding Invitation to clear blockers for lethal combat
Balance mana between casting Colossus, holding up interaction, and fueling Simulacrum Synthesizer.
Use Leyline Axe's sacrifice ability for card draw when you have board control, and use its pump ability only when it secures combat or trades favorably.
Commit Metalwork Colossus when you have removal for their threats or a board wipe ready.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or targeted hate in play.
Use Simulacrum Synthesizer to trade efficiently or protect key artifacts.
late_game: Sacrifice artifacts to return Colossus to hand and repeat the loop if killed
Pivot to Simulacrum Synthesizer token generation
Use Fabrication Foundry to reanimate artifacts and accelerate the combo
Metalwork Colossus with enough +1/+1 counters to be unblockable or too large to remove, or Leyline Axe pumped to lethal.
Simulacrum Synthesizer can also close games by repeatedly fueling Colossus or trading into key threats.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, removal for their threats, and they have no sweepers in hand.
Commit when you can trade your early artifacts for their board wipes or removal.

key_cards.name: Metalwork Colossus
key_cards.notes: Hard-to-kill 10/10 threat with absurd mana value that can be cast for free starting turn four.

key_cards.name: Brass Knuckles
key_cards.notes: Creates a copy on entry, reduces Colossus's mana value by eight, and grants double strike when both are equipped.

key_cards.name: Leyline Axe
key_cards.notes: Enters for free on turn one, reduces cost by four, grants trample and double strike, and acts as a Sol Ring equivalent later.

key_cards.name: Simulacrum Synthesizer
key_cards.notes: Generates tokens from artifacts with mana value 3+, which can be equipped with Leyline Axe for lethal damage.

key_cards.name: Wedding Invitation
key_cards.notes: Removes blockers to enable lethal combat and reduces Colossus's mana value while returning to hand.

key_cards.name: Fabrication Foundry
key_cards.notes: Adds virtual mana, speeds up Brass Knuckles, and reanimates artifacts for longer games or Synthesizer plans.

key_cards.name: Portable Hole
key_cards.notes: Handles red aggro creatures, Prowess, Cori-Steel Cutter, Artist's Talent, and Witch's Oven.

key_cards.name: Perilous Snare
key_cards.notes: Destroys larger permanents while triggering Simulacrum Synthesizer.

key_cards.name: Inventors' Fair
key_cards.notes: Provides recurring life gain and allows searching for specific artifacts or sideboard answers.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing Leyline Axe on turn one to establish early mana and cost reduction.
Hold Wedding Invitation until combat to clear blockers for a lethal Colossus attack.
If Colossus faces removal, sacrifice two artifacts to return it to hand and repeat the combo next turn.
Use Fabrication Foundry's reanimation ability to recover key artifacts in attrition games.
Watch out for artifact hate (Shatter, Disenchant).
Simulacrum Synthesizer's ability is a key resource engine.
Don't overcommit to Colossus without board presence or answers.
Leyline Axe's pump is a tempo tool, not just a finisher.

matchups.advice: Side in Torpor Orb to shut down Sunspine Lynx ETB and Knockout Blow for early creatures.
Side in Unlicensed Hearse to disrupt graveyard recursion and keep Portable Hole for Arclight Phoenix and Artist's Talent.
Side in Glass Casket for small creatures and Perilous Snare for larger threats, while leveraging Inventors' Fair for life gain.
Side in Torpor Orb to block life gain and board interaction, and Containment Priest to handle Angels at instant speed.
Focus on early disruption and use Portable Hole on key combo pieces, while maintaining board presence with Colossus.

Use multiple small threats, sacrifice triggers, and artifact synergies to out-value counterspells. Cast Colossus in response to their counterspells if possible, or use Leyline Axe's draw ability to dig for answers.
Apply early pressure, use artifact hate, and don't let them stabilize. Trade efficiently and use sweepers to reset the board.
Stabilize with early artifacts and removal, then transition to Colossus. Use Leyline Axe's pump to block or trade favorably.
Pressure with early threats, use card advantage to out-grind, and protect Colossus from targeted removal. Use Leyline Axe's draw to dig past their answers.
Win by out-resourcing, using better sequencing, and targeting their key artifacts with removal. Use Simulacrum Synthesizer to fuel Colossus faster or trade efficiently.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Supreme Verdict), targeted removal (e.g., Path to Exile, Doom Blade), and combo finishers.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Once Colossus is on the board, we want to perform a combo-kill: equip it with one of the above cards and attack with Double Strike for 20 damage.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Once Colossus is on the board, we want to perform a combo-kill: equip it with one of the above cards and attack with Double Strike for 20 damage.
Simulacrum Synthesizer is our alternative win condition.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: If your opponent has removal, simply sacrifice some artifacts and return Colossus to your hand, repeating the process the following turn.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Portable Hole is still one of the most effective removals in the current Metagame: it takes care of most Red Aggro creatures, Prowess, Cori-Steel Cutter and Artist's Talent in Izzet Phoenix, and Witch's Oven when necessary.
Glass Casket deals with small creatures that fall outside the scope of Portable Hole , such as Screaming Nemesis , Mayhem Devil , Bonecrusher Giant , and others.
